libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use the Jakarta Activation 2.x package namespace (`jakarta.activation.*`), not the pre-2.0 `javax.activation.*` imports. Be aware that adding the implementation jar introduces duplicate `jakarta.activation` classes; keep tests focused on the target API behavior and use local Gradle/Native Image verification as authoritative.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The API jar POM states it is intended only for compiling and excludes implementation-specific classes. input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The matching implementation artifact contains both the missing `com.sun.activation.registries` classes and duplicate `jakarta.activation` classes, so classpath ordering could affect which duplicate classes are loaded.
If the workflow intentionally wants to test the API artifact in strict compile-only isolation, tests must avoid runtime paths that instantiate MIME/mailcap registries.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0 is an API-only artifact; common Activation runtime paths such as MailcapCommandMap and MimetypesFileTypeMap reference implementation classes under com.sun.activation.registries that are not present in the API jar, so meaningful coverage of MIME/command mapping needs the matching implementation jar.
